diffs.patch:
Index: bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md
===================================================================
--- bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md	
+++ bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,153 @@
+# Routing Read/Write Ledger
+
+## src/hono.ts
+
+- Purpose: Main Hono class file that extends HonoBase and sets up the default router, combining RegExpRouter and TrieRouter in a SmartRouter by default.
+- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, RegExpRouter, SmartRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, HonoOptions
+- Simple note: 34 lines, constructor configures default router as SmartRouter with fallback routers.
+
+## src/hono-base.ts
+
+- Purpose: Base module providing Hono class structure with routing, middleware, error handling, and request/response methods.
+- Names noticed: HonoBase, Hono, HonoOptions, Router, Context, compose, Context, methods, routes, basePath, mount, onError, notFound, fetch, request, fire
+- Simple note: 544 lines, contains core logic for request dispatching, route registration, and middleware composition via router matching.
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
+
+- Purpose: Module export file for PatternRouter implementation.
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter
+- Simple note: 6 lines, simple re-export from router.ts.
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
+
+- Purpose: PatternRouter implementation that compiles routes to regular expressions with named parameter extraction.
+- Names noticed: Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, Route, PatternRouter, emptyParams
+- Simple note: 60 lines, adds routes by converting paths to regex patterns with named capture groups, matches paths using regex exec.
+
+## src/router/linear-router/index.ts
+
+- Purpose: Module export file for LinearRouter implementation.
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter
+- Simple note: 6 lines, simple re-export from router.ts.
+
+## src/router/linear-router/router.ts
+
+- Purpose: Sequential linear router that checks routes in registration order with support for wildcards and named parameters.
+- Names noticed: Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, LinearRouter, emptyParams, ROUTES_LOOP, RegexMatchArrayWithIndices
+- Simple note: 144 lines, loops through all routes sequentially, handles static paths, wildcards (*), and named parameters (:label) with optional regex patterns.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts
+
+- Purpose: Module export file for RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter.
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
+- Simple note: 7 lines, exports router class and prepared router utilities for serialization.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
+
+- Purpose: RegExpRouter implementation that builds a Trie from routes and generates a single comprehensive regular expression for matching.
+- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, match, emptyParam, PATH_ERROR, HandlerWithMetadata, RegExpRouter, buildWildcardRegExp, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, findMiddleware, nullMatcher, wildcardRegExpCache, buildRegExp, Trie
+- Simple note: 252 lines, separates route storage from matching by building matchers at match time, handles middleware with regex pattern matching, builds Trie structure.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts
+
+- Purpose: Defines matcher types and implements the match function for RegExpRouter.
+- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match
+- Simple note: 33 lines, match function builds all matchers on first call and caches them, returns result with param stash mapping.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts
+
+- Purpose: PreparedRegExpRouter for pre-building matchers at application initialization time, enabling faster subsequent matches.
+- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, MatcherMap, RelocateMap, serializeInitParams, buildInitParams
+- Simple note: 157 lines, pre-builds matcher map and relocate map for paths, supports serialization of matched routes and regex patterns.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts
+
+- Purpose: Node class for building the Trie structure from route paths.
+- Names noticed: Context, ParamAssocArray, Node, PATH_ERROR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, regExpMetaChars, compareKey, buildRegExpStr
+- Simple note: 136 lines, recursive Node class with children map, inserts tokens into Trie, builds regex string from node structure.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts
+
+- Purpose: Trie class that manages Node structures and generates the final combined regular expression.
+- Names noticed: ReplacementMap, Trie, Context, Node, ParamAssocArray
+- Simple note: 74 lines, wraps Node insertion and builds combined regex with capture group mapping and handler index placement.
+
+## src/router/smart-router/index.ts
+
+- Purpose: Module export file for SmartRouter implementation.
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter
+- Simple note: 6 lines, simple re-export from router.ts.
+
+## src/router/smart-router/router.ts
+
+- Purpose: SmartRouter that tries multiple routers in sequence and falls back to the first one that successfully matches.
+- Names noticed: Result, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, SmartRouter, activeRouter
+- Simple note: 71 lines, adds routes to all routers, tries each router's match method, switches to first router that succeeds.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/index.ts
+
+- Purpose: Module export file for TrieRouter implementation.
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter
+- Simple note: 6 lines, simple re-export from router.ts.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/router.ts
+
+- Purpose: TrieRouter implementation using a node tree for fast exact and parameterized path matching.
+- Names noticed: Result, Router, checkOptionalParameter, Node, TrieRouter
+- Simple note: 29 lines, wraps Node class for search operations, splits path into parts and queries the node tree.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/node.ts
+
+- Purpose: Tree node structure for TrieRouter with method/handler storage and pattern matching.
+- Names noticed: Params, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath
+- Simple note: 234 lines, recursive node with children, patterns array, methods map, BFS-style search through path parts with pattern regex matching.
+
+## src/common.case.test.ts
+
+- Purpose: Shared test helper for router unit tests using Vitest framework.
+- Names noticed: RunnerTestSuite, skip, runTest, match, Router, Params, ParamIndexMap
+- Simple note: 883 lines, provides common test suites for all routers including basic usage, reserved words, complex patterns, wildcards, optional parameters, multi-match, duplicate params.
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
+
+- Purpose: Unit tests for PatternRouter including common cases and router-specific behavior.
+- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, PatternRouter, runTest, Duplicate param name, Trailing slash
+- Simple note: 42 lines, skips tests for unsupported patterns, verifies trailing slash handling differs from other routers.
+
+## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
+
+- Purpose: Unit tests for LinearRouter verifying sequential matching behavior.
+- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, LinearRouter, runTest, params, Trailing slash, Skip part
+- Simple note: 60 lines, skips tests for label+wildcard combinations, verifies trailing slash handling.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
+
+- Purpose: Unit tests for RegExpRouter verifying regex-based matching behavior.
+- Names noticed: ParamStash, UnsupportedPathError, RegExpRouter, Return value type, UnsupportedPathError, Ambiguous, static and dynamic, parent, child, long label
+- Simple note: 141 lines, throws UnsupportedPathError for ambiguous routes, tests return type with param stash.
+
+## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
+
+- Purpose: Unit tests for SmartRouter that wraps RegExpRouter + TrieRouter combination.
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, SmartRouter, runTest
+- Simple note: 13 lines, uses common.test.ts suite with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter fallback stack.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts
+
+- Purpose: Unit tests for TrieRouter verifying tree-based pattern matching.
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest
+- Simple note: 8 lines, uses comprehensive common.test.ts suite for TrieRouter.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts
+
+- Purpose: Unit tests for Node class used by TrieRouter covering all edge cases.
+- Names notice: Node, insert, search, Root Node, Basic Usage, Name path, Named params and wildcard, Wildcard, Regexp, All, Special Wildcard, Multi match, Sort Order, Duplicate param name
+- Simple note: 811 lines, exhaustive Node class tests including root behavior, wildcards, regex patterns, multi-match ordering, duplicate params, sort order.
+
+## Cross-File Checklist
+
+- pattern-router: src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
+- linear-router: src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
+- reg-exp-router: src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
+- smart-router: src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
+- trie-router: src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

parts.text:
"Benchmark task: routing-read-write-ledger
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Create a new file at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md. This is a read/write endurance task, not a clever debugging task. First create the bench-artifacts directory if it does not exist. Then read every file in this exact list before writing the final document: src/hono.ts, src/hono-base.ts, src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/common.case.test.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts. The output file must be long but mechanically simple. Start with a title '# Routing Read/Write Ledger'. For each listed file, add a section whose heading is exactly '## <file path>'. Under each heading write three bullets: '- Purpose: ...', '- Names noticed: ...', and '- Simple note: ...'. Keep the notes plain and safe; do not change source code. After the per-file sections, add '## Cross-File Checklist' with one bullet per router family: pattern-router, linear-router, reg-exp-router, smart-router, trie-router. Include exact file paths in the final answer and report that only bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md was created.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer."

This is a long task asking me to:
1. Create a bench-artifacts directory
2. Read multiple files from the routing system
3. Write a ledger document at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md

Let me start by creating the directory and reading all the files listed. I'll need to batch these reads efficiently.
